name: scubatrace

metadata.name: scubatrace
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)

classes.name: scubatrace.project.Project

methods.name: scubatrace.project.Project.__int__
documentation:
Initialize the project with the given path and language.

Args:
    path (str): The file path of the project.
    language (type[language.Language]): The programming language used in the project.

methods.name: scubatrace.project.Project.files
documentation:
Retrieves a dictionary of files in the project directory that match the specified language extensions.

This method walks through the directory tree starting from the project's path and collects files
that have extensions matching the language's extensions. It then creates instances of the appropriate
file class (CFile, CPPFile, JavaFile) based on the language and stores them in a dictionary.

Returns:
    dict[str, File]: A dictionary where the keys are relative file paths and the values are instances
                     of the corresponding file class (CFile, CPPFile, JavaFile).

methods.name: scubatrace.project.Project.functions
documentation:
Retrieve a list of all functions from the files in the project.

This method iterates over all files in the project and collects
all functions defined in those files.

Returns:
    list[Function]: A list of Function objects from all files in the project.
Represents a programming project with a specified path and language.

classes.name: scubatrace.project.CProject
documentation:
Representing a C language project.

Attributes:
    path (str): The file path to the project.

classes.name: scubatrace.file.File

methods.name: scubatrace.file.File.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a new instance of the class.

Args:
    path (str): The file path.
    project (Project): The project associated with this instance.

methods.name: scubatrace.file.File.abspath
documentation:
Returns the absolute path of the file.

Returns:
    str: The absolute path of the file.

methods.name: scubatrace.file.File.relpath
documentation:
Returns the relative path of the file with respect to the project directory.

The method removes the project directory path from the file's absolute path,
leaving only the relative path.

Returns:
    str: The relative path of the file.

methods.name: scubatrace.file.File.text
documentation:
Reads the content of the file at the given path and returns it as a string.

Returns:
    str: The content of the file.

Represents a file in a project.

Attributes:
    _path (str): The file path.
    project (Project): The project to which the file belongs.

methods.name: scubatrace.statement.Statement.dot_text
documentation: escape the text ':' for dot

methods.name: scubatrace.statement.BlockStatement.dot_text
documentation: return only the first line of the text

classes.name: scubatrace.function.Function

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a new instance of the class.

Args:
    node (Node): The node associated with this instance.
    file (File): The file associated with this instance.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.text
documentation:
Returns the text content of the node.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the node's text is None.

Returns:
    str: The decoded text content of the node.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.dot_text
documentation:
Escapes the text content of the node for use in DOT files.

Returns:
    str: The escaped text content of the node.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.start_line
documentation:
Returns the starting line number of the node.

The line number is determined by the node's start point and is incremented by 1
to convert from a zero-based index to a one-based index.

Returns:
    int: The starting line number of the node.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.end_line
documentation:
Returns the ending line number of the node.

The line number is derived from the node's end_point attribute and is
incremented by 1 to convert from a zero-based index to a one-based index.

Returns:
    int: The ending line number of the node.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.length
documentation:
Calculate the length of the range.

Returns:
    int: The length of the range, calculated as the difference between
    end_line and start_line, plus one.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.lines
documentation:
Generates a dictionary mapping line numbers to their corresponding lines of text.

Returns:
    dict[int, str]: A dictionary where the keys are line numbers (starting from `self.start_line`)
                    and the values are the lines of text from `self.text`.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.body_node
documentation:
Retrieves the body node of the current node.

Returns:
    Node | None: The body node if it exists, otherwise None.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.body_start_line
documentation:
Returns the starting line number of the body of the node.

If the body node is not defined, it returns the starting line number of the node itself.
Otherwise, it returns the starting line number of the body node.

Returns:
    int: The starting line number of the body or the node.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.body_end_line
documentation:
Returns the ending line number of the body of the node.

If the body_node attribute is None, it returns the end_line attribute.
Otherwise, it returns the line number immediately after the end of the body_node.

Returns:
    int: The ending line number of the body.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.statements_by_type
documentation:
Retrieves all statements of a given node type within the function.

Args:
    type (str): The type of statement node to search for.
    recursive (bool): A flag to indicate whether to search recursively within nested blocks

Returns:
    list[Statement]: A list of statements of the given type.

methods.name: scubatrace.function.Function.export_cfg_dot
documentation:
Exports the CFG of the function to a DOT file.

Args:
    path (str): The path to save the DOT file.

Represents a function in the source code with various properties and methods to access its details.

Attributes:
    node (Node): The AST node representing the function.
    file (File): The file in which the function is defined.
